steps.title: Identify what the finding actually shows
steps.content: The passage establishes that saturated feather color is an "honest signal" of fitness because carotenoids both create the color and confer health benefits. Shultz found that certain tanagers use microstructures to mimic deep saturation without eating carotenoids — so color no longer reliably indicates fitness.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong completions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Work through each choice:
  • Choice A — 'individual male tanagers can engage in honest signaling without relying on carotenoid consumption.': contradicts the finding — faking saturation without carotenoids is dishonest, not honest, signaling. ✗
  • Choice B — 'feather microstructures may be less effective than deeply saturated feathers for signaling overall fitness.': effectiveness of the signal is never discussed in the passage; this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — 'scientists have yet to determine why tanagers have a preference for mates with colorful appearances.': the passage is about signal honesty, not about what scientists don't know regarding mate preference. ✗
  • Choice D — 'a male tanager's appearance may function as a dishonest signal of the individual's overall fitness.': directly follows from the finding — if appearance can be faked, the signal is dishonest. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
